tool_name: Atmospheric Storage Tank Venting — Normal Breathing, Liquid Movement & Emergency Fire Relief

inputs.name: Product Flash Point (T_flash)
inputs.note: Gasoline < 0°C; diesel ≈ 55°C; heavy fuel oil ≈ 70–120°C; crude oil varies

inputs.name: Product Reid Vapour Pressure (RVP)
inputs.note: Gasoline ≈ 55–90 kPa; crude ≈ 40–100 kPa; diesel < 10 kPa; water ≈ 2.3 kPa at 20°C
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Tank Outside Diameter (D_tank)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT5 — Precision grinding tolerance: 4-18µm (≈0.5% for Ø10-50mm)

inputs.name: Maximum Product & Ambient Temperature (T_design)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 §4 — Design specification nominal, zero measurement uncertainty

formulas: V_thermal_inbreath_Nm3h = 0.98 * Math.pow(tank_capacity_m3, 0.9) * 0.001 * 1000 / 24   // [Nm³/h] Thermal In-Breathing (Vacuum) — Daily Temperature Drop Reference: API 2000-7th Ed. Table 2 — thermal in-breathing: Y_IB = 0.98V^0.9 (m³/day); hourly rate

RVP_factor = (product_RVP_kpa > 13.8) ? 1.0 + (product_RVP_kpa - 13.8) * 0.01 : 1.0; V_thermal_outbreath_Nm3h = 0.94 * Math.pow(tank_capacity_m3, 0.9) * RVP_factor * 1000 / 24   // [Nm³/h] Thermal Out-Breathing (Pressure) — Daily Temperature Rise Reference: API 2000 Table 3 — thermal out-breathing with RVP correction; Y_OB = 0.94V^0.9 × F_RVP

V_fill_vent_Nm3h = fill_rate_m3h * 1.01; V_draw_vent_Nm3h = draw_rate_m3h * 1.01   // [Nm³/h] Liquid Movement Venting (In & Out) Reference: API 2000 §5.3.2 — liquid movement venting: 1 m³ liquid in/out displaces 1.01 Nm³ vapour (density correction)

V_total_out_Nm3h = V_thermal_outbreath_Nm3h + V_fill_vent_Nm3h; V_total_in_Nm3h = V_thermal_inbreath_Nm3h + V_draw_vent_Nm3h   // [Nm³/h] Total Normal Vent Capacity Required (Pressure & Vacuum) Reference: API 2000 §5.3.3 — normal vent sizing: combine thermal and liquid movement conservatively

A_wetted_m2 = Math.PI * tank_diameter_m * Math.min(9.14, Math.sqrt(tank_capacity_m3 / (Math.PI/4 * Math.pow(tank_diameter_m, 2)))); Q_fire_kw = 43200 * Math.pow(A_wetted_m2, 0.82) / 3600; lambda_evap_kj_kg = 270; rho_vapour = 3.0; V_fire_Nm3h = Q_fire_kw * 3600 / (lambda_evap_kj_kg * rho_vapour)   // [Nm³/h] Emergency Fire Relief Venting (API 2000 §5.4) Reference: API 2000 §5.4.2 Eq.(1): Q_fire = 43200·F·A^0.82 (BTU/hr) / 3600; λ and ρ_vapour for petroleum product

V_governing_out = Math.max(V_total_out_Nm3h, V_fire_Nm3h); V_governing_in = V_total_in_Nm3h; is_emergency_governing = V_fire_Nm3h > V_total_out_Nm3h   // [Nm³/h] Governing Vent Size — Required Flow vs Emergency Reference: Governing capacity: larger of normal vent and emergency fire vent determines PV valve / conservation vent size

V1.error_msg: Flammable vapour at ambient: tank vapour space above flash point — Class I flammable liquid requires NFPA 30 §22.6 emergency vent; flame arrester mandatory

V2.error_msg: Fill rate > 50% of capacity per hour: very high fill velocity — verify anti-static precautions and bottom fill pipe velocity ≤ 1 m/s per API 2003

V3.error_msg: High RVP product at elevated temperature: significant vapour generation — evaluate floating roof tank or nitrogen blanketing to reduce VOC emissions and fire risk

smart_warnings.message: Emergency fire venting governs: install dedicated emergency pressure-relief device per API 2000 §5.4 — PV valve alone may not provide sufficient fire relief capacity
Large vent flow (>5000 Nm³/h): multiple PV valves or large conservation vent required — coordinate with atmospheric dispersion model for vapour cloud assessment
Large flammable product tank: Category I tank per NFPA 30 — secondary containment bunding, foam deluge system, and thermal radiation analysis required
High temperature + high RVP: thermal out-breathing significantly elevated — verify vent device set pressure and flow rating at T_design per EN 14015 §15.4
No liquid movement: vent sized for thermal breathing only — verify fill/draw rates and confirm tank is not a standby tank with infrequent but fast filling

fmea.effect: Flammable vapour at ambient: tank vapour space above flash point — Class I flammable liquid requires NFPA 30 §22.6 emergency vent; flame arrester mandatory
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Fill rate > 50% of capacity per hour: very high fill velocity — verify anti-static precautions and bottom fill pipe velocity ≤ 1 m/s per API 2003
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: High RVP product at elevated temperature: significant vapour generation — evaluate floating roof tank or nitrogen blanketing to reduce VOC emissions and fire risk
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Emergency fire venting governs: install dedicated emergency pressure-relief device per API 2000 §5.4 — PV valve alone may not provide sufficient fire relief capacity
Large vent flow (>5000 Nm³/h): multiple PV valves or large conservation vent required — coordinate with atmospheric dispersion model for vapour cloud assessment
Large flammable product tank: Category I tank per NFPA 30 — secondary containment bunding, foam deluge system, and thermal radiation analysis required
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: product_flash_c < 60 && tank_capacity_m3 > 10000

fmea.failureMode: High temperature + high RVP: thermal out-breathing significantly elevated — verify vent device set pressure and flow rating at T_design per EN 14015 §15.4
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: design_temp_c > 50 && product_RVP_kpa > 60

fmea.failureMode: No liquid movement: vent sized for thermal breathing only — verify fill/draw rates and confirm tank is not a standby tank with infrequent but fast filling
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: fill_rate_m3h === 0 && draw_rate_m3h === 0
